response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically versatile engineer with nearly two decades of software development experience across diverse domains, but he falls well short of the Principal Engineer (AI Strategy) bar. The role demands Director-level IC standing, recognized AI expertise with demonstrable products or platforms, and a track record of advising strategic customers on cloud and AI — none of which are evident. His AI involvement is limited to a six-month Staff Engineer stint ending in a current caregiving gap, with MCP tooling work that, while relevant, does not constitute the depth of AI product leadership or thought leadership this role requires. The absence of an advanced degree in an AI-related field, cloud platform strategy experience, and executive-level stakeholder engagement further widen the gap.
strengths: Nearly 20 years of hands-on software engineering across embedded, backend, mobile, and web stacks demonstrates genuine technical breadth
Recent exposure to AI agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq provides a foothold in agentic AI workflows
Demonstrated entrepreneurial initiative through the GISCollective open-source platform and a consistent community teaching/speaking record
concerns: AI expertise is shallow and recent — limited to six months of MCP tooling work with no recognized AI products, publications, or platform leadership at scale
Seniority trajectory does not reach Director-level IC: titles plateau at Senior/Staff with no evidence of strategic advisory, executive engagement, or org-wide technical influence
Active career gap and no cloud platform strategy experience make the candidate unready for a role centered on advising enterprise customers and setting AI direction
